name: Weighted Referential Activity Dictionary
analyst_summary: WRAD is an automated dictionary scorer that quantifies referential activity — the degree to which language is concrete, specific, clear, and imagery-evoking vs. abstract, vague, fragmented. Each of 707 words in the dictionary has a weight from -1 to +1 (12 discrete values, no zeros). Score is the mean weight across all matched words in the passage. High WRAD = vivid, embodied, sensory language anchored in specifics. Low WRAD = abstract, evaluative, hedged, disembodied language. Useful as a baseline marker across all phases — concreteness predicts emotional engagement and rules out empty performance.
source: Bucci, W. (1997). Psychoanalysis and Cognitive Science: A Multiple Code Theory. Guilford Press. WRAD dictionary maintained at github.com/DAAP/WRAD.

scoring_method.tokenize: Lowercase + split on whitespace + strip punctuation

output_shape.wrad_mean: float in [-1, 1] — mean weight of matched words
output_shape.matched_count: int — tokens that matched the dictionary

interpretation.high_positive: WRAD > 0.3: highly concrete, vivid, specific language
interpretation.negative: WRAD < 0: abstract, evaluative, hedged language dominates
